summary: Re-linked the Dolphin debug library file `src/dolphin/db/db.c` by marking it as `Matching` in `configure.py` and adjusting `__DBExceptionDestination` linkage/conditional compilation. The function was changed from `static` to externally linked in all compiler branches, and the CodeWarrior asm branch now keys off `MWERKS_GEKKO` instead of `MUST_MATCH`.
what_changed: `configure.py` changed the DolphinLib entry for `dolphin/db/db.c` from `Object(NonMatching, "dolphin/db/db.c")` to `Object(Matching, "dolphin/db/db.c")`.
`src/dolphin/db/db.c` changed the asm implementation guard for `__DBExceptionDestination` from `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` to `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO`.
`__DBExceptionDestination` was changed from `static` to non-static in the `MWERKS_GEKKO`, GNUC/PPCGEKKO, and fallback `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` branches.
No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the slice.
smart_moves: The matching-status flip was paired with a minimal source-level linkage fix instead of broad rewrites, keeping the relink targeted.
Using `MWERKS_GEKKO` for the inline assembly branch ties the CodeWarrior-style `asm void` implementation to the compiler/platform condition rather than to the project-level `MUST_MATCH` state.
Removing `static` consistently across all conditional implementations avoids divergent symbol linkage depending on compiler branch.
decomp_lessons: When promoting a Dolphin object from `NonMatching` to `Matching`, check whether local `static` declarations are accidentally hiding symbols that must appear in the linked object.

Do not use `MUST_MATCH` as a substitute for compiler feature detection when selecting compiler-specific inline assembly syntax; prefer explicit compiler/platform macros such as `MWERKS_GEKKO` when that is what the branch actually depends on.
Small linkage qualifiers can be enough to make an otherwise decompiled file re-link cleanly; matching work is not always about rewriting logic.
naming_conventions: Dolphin debug-library functions in this file use double-underscore internal-style names such as `__DBExceptionDestination` and `__DBExceptionDestinationAux`.
The build configuration represents Dolphin library objects with `DolphinLib("db", [Object(Matching, "dolphin/db/db.c")])`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For CodeWarrior/Gekko builds, keep the function as `asm void __DBExceptionDestination(void)` with `nofralloc`, `mfmsr r3`, `ori r3, r3, 0x30`, `mtmsr r3`, and branch to `__DBExceptionDestinationAux`.
For GNUC/PPCGEKKO, keep a C function wrapping inline asm strings for the same MSR update and branch behavior.
Marking the object as `Matching` in `configure.py` was the explicit relink step after the source adjustment.
follow_up_queries: Which external reference or linker symbol required `__DBExceptionDestination` to be non-static?
Are there other Dolphin runtime files still using `MUST_MATCH` where a compiler/platform macro would be more appropriate?
Did the linked map or symbol table change only for `__DBExceptionDestination`, or were there secondary ordering effects?
